title: CenterPoint Energy: Well-Positioned For Ongoing Dividend Growth
text: CenterPoint Energy's shares have underperformed, losing about 10% of their value due to higher interest rates and concerns about cap-ex programs. The company reported adjusted EPS of $0.40, up from $0.32 last year, and expects to earn $1.49-$1.51 for the full year. CenterPoint has simplified its operations around its core Houston utility, which is attractive with its go-forward dividend growth potential.

title: Reasons to Add CenterPoint (CNP) to Your Portfolio Right Now
text: CenterPoint Energy (CNP) makes a strong case for investment, given its well-chalked-out investment plan, regular dividend payments and a stable ROE.

title: 5 Low-Beta Utilities Stocks to Buy as Fed Remains Hawkish
text: Low-beta utility stocks like CenterPoint Energy (CNP), Consolidated Edison (ED), Entergy (ETR), Global Water Resources (GWRS) and NiSource (NI) are safe bets as the Fed remains open to more interest rate hikes.

title: 5 Safe Stocks to Buy Amid Plummeting Consumer Sentiment
text: Consumer sentiment is declining and markets are volatile. Hence, investing in defensive stocks like CenterPoint Energy, Inc. (CNP), NiSource Inc. (NI), Consolidated Edison, Inc. (ED) PepsiCo, Inc. (PEP), and The Procter & Gamble Company (PG) will be a wise idea.

title: CNP vs. SO: Which Stock Should Value Investors Buy Now?
